title: rhamnus lycioides subsp. oleoides
intro: Rhamnus lycioides subsp. oleoides, also known as the Chinese buckthorn or Frangula, is a plant species belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The health benefits of Chinese buckthorn include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues, such as diarrhea, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Chinese buckthorn is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its preparations can be found in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and extracts, for use in traditional medicine and modern herbal remedies.
